name: skeleton
title: Skeleton
description: A placeholder loading indicator that mimics the shape of content yet to load.

docs.overview: Skeleton renders a muted placeholder matching the dimensions and shape of upcoming content. Use it to indicate loading states and reduce perceived latency.
docs.accessibility: Skeleton uses the 'img' accessibility role with a hidden label so screen readers announce it as a loading placeholder without interrupting the content flow.

props.name: width
props.description: Width of the skeleton in pixels

props.name: height
props.description: Height of the skeleton in pixels

examples.name: text
examples.title: Text Skeleton
examples.code:
VStack(8, [
  Skeleton({ variant: 'text', width: 240 }),
  Skeleton({ variant: 'text', width: 180 }),
  Skeleton({ variant: 'text', width: 200 }),
])

examples.name: circular
examples.title: Circular Skeleton
examples.code:
HStack(12, [
  Skeleton({ variant: 'circular', width: 48, height: 48 }),
  VStack(8, [
    Skeleton({ variant: 'text', width: 120 }),
    Skeleton({ variant: 'text', width: 80 }),
  ]),
])

agent.summary: Skeleton renders a muted placeholder shape to indicate loading content.
useWhen: Avatar placeholders while loading profile data
layoutHints: Match skeleton dimensions to the content it replaces
Use the text variant for paragraph and heading placeholders
Use the circular variant for avatar and icon placeholders
accessibilityChecklist: Skeleton uses role='img' with a hidden label
Replace skeletons with real content once loaded
Announce loading state to screen readers when appropriate
